content:
# Matthew Chapter 26 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_026_page_001.jpg, MAT_026_page_002.jpg, MAT_026_page_003.jpg

### The Plot Against Jesus

1. When Jesus had finished saying all these things, he told his disciples,

2. "As you know, Passover begins in two days, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified."

3. At that same time, the chief priests and the elders were meeting at the palace of the high priest, Caiaphas,

4. plotting to have Jesus arrested secretly and killed.

5. "But not during the Passover festival," they agreed, "or there will be a riot."

### A Woman Anoints Jesus

6. Meanwhile, Jesus was in Bethany at the home of Simon, a man who had leprosy.

7. While he was eating, a woman came with a beautiful alabaster jar of expensive perfume and poured it over his head.

8. The disciples were outraged. "What a waste!" they said.

9. "This perfume could have been sold for a fortune and the money given to the poor."

10. But Jesus, aware of their grumbling, said, "Why are you bothering her? She has done something wonderful for me.

11. You will always have the poor among you, but you won't always have me.

12. When she poured this perfume on my body, she was preparing me for burial.

13. I tell you the truth, wherever this good news is proclaimed throughout the world, what she has done will be remembered and discussed."

### Judas Agrees to Betray Jesus

14. Then one of the twelve disciples — Judas Iscariot — went to the chief priests

15. and asked, "How much will you pay me to hand him over to you?" They counted out thirty pieces of silver.

16. From then on, Judas began looking for the right moment to betray him.

### The Last Supper

17. On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, "Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal for you?"

18. He replied, "Go to a certain man in the city and tell him, 'The Teacher says: my time has come. I will celebrate the Passover at your house with my disciples.'"

19. So the disciples did as Jesus told them and prepared the Passover meal.

20. That evening, Jesus sat down to eat with the twelve.

21. During the meal he said, "I tell you the truth, one of you will betray me."

22. Deeply distressed, each one asked, "Surely not I, Lord?"

23. He answered, "One who has dipped his hand into the bowl with me will betray me.

24. The Son of Man must go as the Scriptures say he will. But how terrible it will be for the one who betrays him. It would be better for that man if he had never been born."

25. Judas, the one who would betray him, asked, "Surely not I, Rabbi?" Jesus told him, "You have said it yourself."

26. As they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks, broke it, and handed it to his disciples, saying, "Take this and eat it, for this is my body."

27. Then he took a cup of wine, gave thanks to God, and passed it to them, saying, "Each of you, drink from this,

28. for this is my blood of the covenant, poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.

29. I tell you, I will not drink wine again until the day I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom."

30. After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

### Jesus Predicts Peter's Denial

31. Then Jesus told them, "Tonight all of you will abandon me. For the Scriptures say, 'God will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.'

32. But after I have been raised from the dead, I will go ahead of you to Galilee."

33. Peter declared, "Even if everyone else abandons you, I never will."

34. Jesus replied, "I tell you the truth, Peter — this very night, before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times."

35. "Even if I have to die with you, I will never deny you!" Peter insisted. And all the other disciples said the same.

### Gethsemane

36. Then Jesus brought them to a place called Gethsemane, and he told his disciples, "Sit here while I go over there to pray."

37. He took Peter and the two sons of Zebedee with him, and he became overwhelmed with grief.

38. He told them, "My soul is crushed with grief to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with me."

39. He went a little farther and fell face down on the ground, praying, "My Father! If it is possible, let this cup of suffering pass from me. Yet I want your will to be done, not mine."

40. Then he returned to the disciples and found them asleep. He said to Peter, "Couldn't you stay awake with me even one hour?

41. Keep watch and pray, so that you won't give in to temptation. The spirit is willing, but the body is weak."

42. He went away a second time and prayed, "My Father! If this cup cannot be taken away unless I drink it, your will be done."

43. When he returned, he again found them sleeping — their eyes were heavy.

44. So he left them and prayed a third time, saying the same thing again.

45. Then he returned to the disciples and said, "Go ahead and sleep. Have your rest. But look — the time has come. The Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

46. Get up, let's go. Look, my betrayer is here!"

### The Arrest

47. While Jesus was still speaking, Judas arrived — one of the twelve — with a large mob armed with swords and clubs, sent by the chief priests and the elders.

48. The betrayer had arranged a signal with them: "The one I greet with a kiss is the man. Arrest him."

49. So Judas went straight to Jesus. "Greetings, Rabbi!" he said, and kissed him.

50. Jesus said, "Friend, do what you came to do." Then the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.

51. One of Jesus' companions reached for his sword, drew it, and slashed off the ear of the high priest's servant.

52. "Put away your sword," Jesus told him. "Those who use the sword will die by the sword.

53. Don't you realize that I could ask my Father, and he would instantly send more than twelve legions of angels?

54. But then how would the Scriptures be fulfilled that say it must happen this way?"

55. Then Jesus said to the crowd, "Am I some dangerous rebel, that you had to come with swords and clubs to arrest me? Day after day I sat teaching in the temple, and you didn't arrest me then.

56. But this is all happening to fulfill the words of the prophets." Then all the disciples deserted him and ran away.

### Jesus Before the Sanhedrin

57. Those who had arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest, where the teachers of the law and the elders had assembled.

58. Peter followed at a distance, as far as the courtyard of the high priest. He went inside and sat with the guards to see how it would all end.

59. The chief priests and the entire high council were trying to find witnesses who would lie about Jesus so they could put him to death.

60. But even though many false witnesses came forward, they couldn't find any consistent testimony. Finally, two men stepped forward

61. and declared, "This man said, 'I am able to destroy the temple of God and rebuild it in three days.'"

62. Then the high priest stood up and said to Jesus, "Aren't you going to answer? What about all this testimony against you?"

63. But Jesus remained silent. Then the high priest said to him, "I demand under oath by the living God — tell us if you are the Messiah, the Son of God."

64. Jesus replied, "You have said it. And I tell you this: in the future you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Mighty One and coming on the clouds of heaven."

65. Then the high priest tore his robes and shouted, "Blasphemy! Why do we need other witnesses? You have all heard his blasphemy!

66. What is your verdict?" They answered, "He deserves to die!"

67. Then they spat in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him,

68. jeering, "Prophesy to us, Messiah! Who hit you?"

### Peter Denies Jesus

69. Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant girl came up to him and said, "You were with Jesus the Galilean too."

70. But Peter denied it in front of everyone. "I don't know what you're talking about," he said.

71. When he went out to the gateway, another servant girl noticed him and said to the others, "This man was with Jesus of Nazareth."

72. Again Peter denied it, this time with an oath. "I don't even know the man," he swore.

73. A little later, the bystanders came over and said to Peter, "You must be one of them — your accent gives you away."

74. Then Peter began to curse and swear. "I don't know the man!" And immediately a rooster crowed.

75. Then Peter remembered what Jesus had said: "Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times." And he went outside and wept bitterly.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 7 — The anointing woman:** She is unnamed in Matthew and Mark — but what she did is more famous than most named characters in the Bible. Jesus says her story will be told wherever the gospel is proclaimed (v.13). This is fulfilled in the act of reading this text.

**Verse 15 — "Thirty pieces of silver":** The price of a slave in Exodus 21:32. Zechariah 11:12-13 prophesied this exact amount. The chief priests are literally buying Jesus at slave prices — the most insulting valuation imaginable for the one they will later charge with claiming to be God's Son.

**Verse 28 — "Blood of the covenant":** No word "new" in the Sinaiticus manuscript. Jesus deliberately echoes Moses at Sinai (Exodus 24:8): "Behold the blood of the covenant." He is establishing a new Sinai, a new covenant — sealed not with the blood of animals but with his own. Every communion table reenacts this moment.

**Verse 39 — "Let this cup pass from me":** Jesus is not play-acting. He genuinely doesn't want to die. The cup is a biblical metaphor for God's wrath (Isaiah 51:17, Jeremiah 25:15). What terrifies Jesus isn't the physical pain — it's becoming the vessel that absorbs divine judgment. His prayer moves from "if possible, take it away" to "your will be done." This is the real battle — Gethsemane, not Golgotha, is where the victory is won.

**Verse 50 — "Friend, do what you came to do":** The word "friend" (ἑταῖρε) is not the warm word for friend (φίλος). It's formal, even cold — the same word used for the man without a wedding garment in 22:12. Jesus knows exactly what Judas is doing. He doesn't resist. He gives permission.

**Verses 69-75 — Peter's three denials:** The escalation is brutal: first a casual denial, then an oath, then cursing and swearing. Peter, who just hours ago swore he would die with Jesus, can't even admit knowing him to a servant girl. The rooster crow shatters him. His bitter weeping is the sound of a man discovering who he really is — and it is the beginning of his redemption.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
